translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The beginning of the Good News of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As it is written in the prophets, “Behold,  I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make ready the way of the Lord! Make his paths straight!’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: John came baptizing  in the wilderness and preaching the baptism of repentance for forgiveness of sins.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the country of Judea and all those of Jerusalem went out to him. They were baptized by him in the Jordan river, confessing their sins.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: John was clothed with camel’s hair and a leather belt around his waist. He ate locusts and wild honey.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He preached, saying, “After me comes he who is mightier than I, the thong of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and loosen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I baptized you in  water, but he will baptize you in the Holy Spirit.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In those days, Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized by John in the Jordan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately coming up from the water, he saw the heavens parting, and the Spirit descending on him like a dove.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A voice came out of the sky, “You are my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately the Spirit drove him out into the wilderness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was there in the wilderness forty days tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals; and the angels were serving him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now after John was taken into custody, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the Good News of God’s Kingdom,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and saying, “The time is fulfilled, and God’s Kingdom is at hand! Repent, and believe in the Good News.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Passing along by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew the brother of Simon casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to them, “Come after me, and I will make you into fishers for men.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately they left their nets, and followed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Going on a little further from there, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John, his brother, who were also in the boat mending the nets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately he called them, and they left their father, Zebedee, in the boat with the hired servants, and went after him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath day he entered into the synagogue and taught.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were astonished at his teaching, for he taught them as having authority, and not as the scribes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit, and he cried out,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying, “Ha! What do we have to do with you, Jesus, you Nazarene? Have you come to destroy us? I know you who you are: the Holy One of God!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be quiet, and come out of him!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The unclean spirit, convulsing him and crying with a loud voice, came out of him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, “What is this? A new teaching? For with authority he commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The report of him went out immediately everywhere into all the region of Galilee and its surrounding area.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately, when they had come out of the synagogue, they came into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick with a fever, and immediately they told him about her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came and took her by the hand, and raised her up. The fever left her, and she served them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At evening, when the sun had set, they brought to him all who were sick, and those who were possessed by demons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the city was gathered together at the door.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He healed many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons. He didn’t allow the demons to speak, because they knew him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Early in the morning, while it was still dark, he rose up and went out, and departed into a deserted place, and prayed there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Simon and those who were with him followed after him;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they found him, and told him, “Everyone is looking for you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Let’s go elsewhere into the next towns, that I may preach there also, because I came out for this reason.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went into their synagogues throughout all Galilee, preaching and casting out demons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A leper came to him, begging him, kneeling down to him, and saying to him, “If you want to, you can make me clean.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Being moved with compassion, he stretched out his hand, and touched him, and said to him, “I want to. Be made clean.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had said this, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was made clean.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He strictly warned him, and immediately sent him out,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and said to him, “See you say nothing to anybody, but go show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing the things which Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he went out, and began to proclaim it much, and to spread about the matter, so that Jesus could no more openly enter into a city, but was outside in desert places: and they came to him from everywhere.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he entered again into Capernaum after some days, it was heard that he was in the house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately many were gathered together, so that there was no more room, not even around the door; and he spoke the word to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Four people came, carrying a paralytic to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they could not come near to him for the crowd, they removed the roof where he was. When they had broken it up, they let down the mat that the paralytic was lying on.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus, seeing their faith, said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But there were some of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Why does this man speak blasphemies like that? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, said to them, “Why do you reason these things in your hearts?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Which is easier, to tell the paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven;’ or to say, ‘Arise, and take up your bed, and walk?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” — he said to the paralytic —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I tell you, arise, take up your mat, and go to your house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He arose, and immediately took up the mat, and went out in front of them all; so that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, “We never saw anything like this!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went out again by the seaside. All the multitude came to him, and he taught them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he passed by, he saw Levi, the son of Alphaeus, sitting at the tax office, and he said to him, “Follow me.” And he arose and followed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was reclining at the table in his house, and many tax collectors and sinners sat down with Jesus and his disciples, for there were many, and they followed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The scribes and the Pharisees, when they saw that he was eating with the sinners and tax collectors, said to his disciples, “Why is it that he eats and drinks with tax collectors and sinners?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jesus heard it, he said to them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting, and they came and asked him, “Why do John’s disciples and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but your disciples don’t fast?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to them, “Can the groomsmen fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them, they can’t fast.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then will they fast in that day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, or else the patch shrinks and the new tears away from the old, and a worse hole is made.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No one puts new wine into old wineskins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and the wine pours out, and the skins will be destroyed; but they put new wine into fresh wineskins.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was going on the Sabbath day through the grain fields, and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of grain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees said to him, “Behold, why do they do that which is not lawful on the Sabbath day?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Did you never read what David did, when he had need, and was hungry — he, and those who were with him?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: How he entered into God’s house when Abiathar was high priest, and ate the show bread, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests, and gave also to those who were with him?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He entered again into the synagogue, and there was a man there who had his hand withered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They watched him, whether he would heal him on the Sabbath day, that they might accuse him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the man who had his hand withered, “Stand up.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Is it lawful on the Sabbath day to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?” But they were silent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had looked around at them with anger, being grieved at the hardening of their hearts, he said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.”  He stretched it out, and his hand was restored as healthy as the other.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees went out, and immediately conspired with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus withdrew to the sea with his disciples, and a great multitude followed him from Galilee, from Judea,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: from Jerusalem, from Idumaea, beyond the Jordan, and those from around Tyre and Sidon. A great multitude, hearing what great things he did, came to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He spoke to his disciples that a little boat should stay near him because of the crowd, so that they wouldn’t press on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he had healed many, so that as many as had diseases pressed on him that they might touch him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The unclean spirits, whenever they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, “You are the Son of God!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sternly warned them that they should not make him known.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went up into the mountain, and called to himself those whom he wanted, and they went to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He appointed twelve, that they might be with him, and that he might send them out to preach,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and to have authority to heal sicknesses and to cast out demons:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Simon, to whom he gave the name Peter;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: James the son of Zebedee; John, the brother of James, and he called them Boanerges, which means, Sons of Thunder;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Andrew; Philip; Bartholomew; Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Thaddaeus; Simon the Zealot;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him. He came into a house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The multitude came together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When his friends heard it, they went out to seize him: for they said, “He is insane.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, “He has Beelzebul,” and, “By the prince of the demons he casts out the demons.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He summoned them, and said to them in parables, “How can Satan cast out Satan?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If Satan has risen up against himself, and is divided, he can’t stand, but has an end.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But no one can enter into the house of the strong man to plunder, unless he first binds the strong man; and then he will plunder his house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Most certainly I tell you, all sins of the descendants of man will be forgiven, including their blasphemies with which they may blaspheme;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but whoever may blaspheme against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is subject to eternal condemnation.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: — because they said, “He has an unclean spirit.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His mother and his brothers came, and standing outside, they sent to him, calling him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A multitude was sitting around him, and they told him, “Behold, your mother, your brothers, and your sisters  are outside looking for you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered them, “Who are my mother and my brothers?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Looking around at those who sat around him, he said, “Behold, my mother and my brothers!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For whoever does the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again he began to teach by the seaside. A great multitude was gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat in the sea, and sat down. All the multitude were on the land by the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He taught them many things in parables, and told them in his teaching,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Listen! Behold, the farmer went out to sow,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and as he sowed, some seed fell by the road, and the birds  came and devoured it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Others fell on the rocky ground, where it had little soil, and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of soil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the sun had risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Others fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Others fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing. Some produced thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times as much.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Whoever has ears to hear, let him hear.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he was alone, those who were around him with the twelve asked him about the parables.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “To you is given the mystery of God’s Kingdom, but to those who are outside, all things are done in parables,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that ‘seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest perhaps they should turn again, and their sins should be forgiven them.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Don’t you understand this parable? How will you understand all of the parables?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The farmer sows the word.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The ones by the road are the ones where the word is sown; and when they have heard, immediately Satan comes, and takes away the word which has been sown in them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These in the same way are those who are sown on the rocky places, who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with joy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They have no root in themselves, but are short-lived. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they stumble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Others are those who are sown among the thorns. These are those who have heard the word,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the cares of this age, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those which were sown on the good ground are those who hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit, some thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Is the lamp brought to be put under a basket   or under a bed? Isn’t it put on a stand?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For there is nothing hidden, except that it should be made known; neither was anything made secret, but that it should come to light.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If any man has ears to hear, let him hear.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Take heed what you hear. With whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you, and more will be given to you who hear.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For whoever has, to him will more be given, and he who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “God’s Kingdom is as if a man should cast seed on the earth,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, he doesn’t know how.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the earth bears fruit: first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when the fruit is ripe, immediately he puts in the sickle, because the harvest has come.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “How will we liken God’s Kingdom? Or with what parable will we illustrate it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It’s like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, though it is less than all the seeds that are on the earth,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: yet when it is sown, grows up, and becomes greater than all the herbs, and puts out great branches, so that the birds of the sky can lodge under its shadow.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Without a parable he didn’t speak to them; but privately to his own disciples he explained everything.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, “Let’s go over to the other side.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Leaving the multitude, they took him with them, even as he was, in the boat. Other small boats were also with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A big wind storm arose, and the waves beat into the boat, so much that the boat was already filled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion, and they woke him up, and told him, “Teacher, don’t you care that we are dying?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He awoke, and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Peace! Be still!” The wind ceased, and there was a great calm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Why are you so afraid? How is it that you have no faith?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were greatly afraid, and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came to the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had come out of the boat, immediately a man with an unclean spirit met him out of the tombs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He lived in the tombs. Nobody could bind him any more, not even with chains,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been torn apart by him, and the fetters broken in pieces. Nobody had the strength to tame him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Always, night and day, in the tombs and in the mountains, he was crying out, and cutting himself with stones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he saw Jesus from afar, he ran and bowed down to him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and crying out with a loud voice, he said, “What have I to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, don’t torment me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he said to him, “Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He asked him, “What is your name?” He said to him, “My name is Legion, for we are many.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He begged him much that he would not send them away out of the country.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now on the mountainside there was a great herd of pigs feeding.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the demons begged him, saying, “Send us into the pigs, that we may enter into them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At once Jesus gave them permission. The unclean spirits came out and entered into the pigs. The herd of about two thousand rushed down the steep bank into the sea, and they were drowned in the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who fed them fled, and told it in the city and in the country. The people came to see what it was that had happened.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came to Jesus, and saw him who had been possessed by demons sitting, clothed, and in his right mind, even him who had the legion; and they were afraid.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who saw it declared to them what happened to him who was possessed by demons, and about the pigs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They began to beg him to depart from their region.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he was entering into the boat, he who had been possessed by demons begged him that he might be with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He didn’t allow him, but said to him, “Go to your house, to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done for you, and how he had mercy on you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went his way, and began to proclaim in Decapolis how Jesus had done great things for him, and everyone marveled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jesus had crossed back over in the boat to the other side, a great multitude was gathered to him; and he was by the sea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name, came; and seeing him, he fell at his feet,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and begged him much, saying, “My little daughter is at the point of death. Please come and lay your hands on her, that she may be made healthy, and live.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went with him, and a great multitude followed him, and they pressed upon him on all sides.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A certain woman, who had an issue of blood for twelve years,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and had suffered many things by many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was no better, but rather grew worse,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: having heard the things concerning Jesus, came up behind him in the crowd, and touched his clothes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For she said, “If I just touch his clothes, I will be made well.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately the flow of her blood was dried up, and she felt in her body that she was healed of her affliction.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in himself that the power had gone out from him, turned around in the crowd, and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His disciples said to him, “You see the multitude pressing against you, and you say, ‘Who touched me?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He looked around to see her who had done this thing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the woman, fearing and trembling, knowing what had been done to her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace, and be cured of your disease.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While he was still speaking, people came from the synagogue ruler’s house saying, “Your daughter is dead. Why bother the Teacher any more?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, when he heard the message spoken, immediately said to the ruler of the synagogue, “Don’t be afraid, only believe.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He allowed no one to follow him, except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came to the synagogue ruler’s house, and he saw an uproar, weeping, and great wailing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had entered in, he said to them, “Why do you make an uproar and weep? The child is not dead, but is asleep.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They ridiculed him. But he, having put them all out, took the father of the child, her mother, and those who were with him, and went in where the child was lying.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Taking the child by the hand, he said to her, “Talitha cumi!”  which means, being interpreted, “Girl, I tell you, get up!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately the girl rose up and walked, for she was twelve years old. They were amazed with great amazement.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He strictly ordered them that no one should know this, and commanded that something should be given to her to eat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went out from there. He came into his own country, and his disciples followed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the Sabbath had come, he began to teach in the synagogue, and many hearing him were astonished, saying, “Where did this man get these things?” and, “What is the wisdom that is given to this man, that such mighty works come about by his hands?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Isn’t this the carpenter, the son of Mary, and brother of James, Joses, Judah, and Simon? Aren’t his sisters here with us?” They were offended at him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor, except in his own country, and among his own relatives, and in his own house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He could do no mighty work there, except that he laid his hands on a few sick people, and healed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He marveled because of their unbelief. He went around the villages teaching.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called to himself the twelve, and began to send them out two by two; and he gave them authority over the unclean spirits.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey, except a staff only: no bread, no wallet, no money in their purse,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but to wear sandals, and not put on two tunics.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Wherever you enter into a house, stay there until you depart from there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever will not receive you nor hear you, as you depart from there, shake off the dust that is under your feet for a testimony against them. Assuredly, I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went out and preached that people should repent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They cast out many demons, and anointed many with oil who were sick, and healed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: King Herod heard this, for his name had become known, and he said, “John the Baptizer has risen from the dead, and therefore these powers are at work in him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But others said, “He is Elijah.” Others said, “He is a prophet, or like one of the prophets.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Herod, when he heard this, said, “This is John, whom I beheaded. He has risen from the dead.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Herod himself had sent out and arrested John, and bound him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife, for he had married her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For John said to Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Herodias set herself against him, and desired to kill him, but she couldn’t,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and kept him safe. When he heard him, he did many things, and he heard him gladly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then a convenient day came, that Herod on his birthday made a supper for his nobles, the high officers, and the chief men of Galilee.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the daughter of Herodias herself came in and danced, she pleased Herod and those sitting with him. The king said to the young lady, “Ask me whatever you want, and I will give it to you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He swore to her, “Whatever you shall ask of me, I will give you, up to half of my kingdom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She went out, and said to her mother, “What shall I ask?” She said, “The head of John the Baptizer.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She came in immediately with haste to the king, and asked, “I want you to give me right now the head of John the Baptizer on a platter.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The king was exceedingly sorry, but for the sake of his oaths, and of his dinner guests, he didn’t wish to refuse her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately the king sent out a soldier of his guard, and commanded to bring John’s head, and he went and beheaded him in the prison,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the young lady; and the young lady gave it to her mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When his disciples heard this, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The apostles gathered themselves together to Jesus, and they told him all things, whatever they had done, and whatever they had taught.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “You come apart into a deserted place, and rest awhile.” For there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went away in the boat to a deserted place by themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They  saw them going, and many recognized him and ran there on foot from all the cities. They arrived before them and came together to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus came out, saw a great multitude, and he had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd, and he began to teach them many things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When it was late in the day, his disciples came to him, and said, “This place is deserted, and it is late in the day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Send them away, that they may go into the surrounding country and villages, and buy themselves bread, for they have nothing to eat.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.” They asked him, “Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii  worth of bread, and give them something to eat?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “How many loaves do you have? Go see.” When they knew, they said, “Five, and two fish.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He commanded them that everyone should sit down in groups on the green grass.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They sat down in ranks, by hundreds and by fifties.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed and broke the loaves, and he gave to his disciples to set before them, and he divided the two fish among them all.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They all ate, and were filled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and also of the fish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who ate the loaves were  five thousand men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat, and to go ahead to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he himself sent the multitude away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After he had taken leave of them, he went up the mountain to pray.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When evening had come, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and he was alone on the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seeing them distressed in rowing, for the wind was contrary to them, about the fourth watch of the night he came to them, walking on the sea,  and he would have passed by them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but they, when they saw him walking on the sea, supposed that it was a ghost, and cried out;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for they all saw him, and were troubled. But he immediately spoke with them, and said to them, “Cheer up! It is I!  Don’t be afraid.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He got into the boat with them; and the wind ceased, and they were very amazed among themselves, and marveled;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for they hadn’t understood about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret, and moored to the shore.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had come out of the boat, immediately the people recognized him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and ran around that whole region, and began to bring those who were sick, on their mats, to where they heard he was.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wherever he entered, into villages, or into cities, or into the country, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged him that they might touch just the fringe  of his garment; and as many as touched him were made well.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then the Pharisees and some of the scribes gathered together to him, having come from Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when they saw some of his disciples eating bread with defiled, that is unwashed, hands, they found fault.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews, don’t eat unless they wash their hands and forearms, holding to the tradition of the elders.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They don’t eat when they come from the marketplace unless they bathe themselves, and there are many other things, which they have received to hold to: washings of cups, pitchers, bronze vessels, and couches.)

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why don’t your disciples walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat their bread with unwashed hands?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered them, “Well did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written,  ‘This people honors me with their lips,  but their heart is far from me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But they worship me in vain,  teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “For you set aside the commandment of God, and hold tightly to the tradition of men — the washing of pitchers and cups, and you do many other such things.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Full well do you reject the commandment of God, that you may keep your tradition.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother;’  and, ‘He who speaks evil of father or mother, let him be put to death.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you say, ‘If a man tells his father or his mother, “Whatever profit you might have received from me is Corban,  that is to say, given to God”;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then you no longer allow him to do anything for his father or his mother,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: making void the word of God by your tradition, which you have handed down. You do many things like this.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called all the multitude to himself, and said to them, “Hear me, all of you, and understand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is nothing from outside of the man, that going into him can defile him; but the things which proceed out of the man are those that defile the man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had entered into a house away from the multitude, his disciples asked him about the parable.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Are you also without understanding? Don’t you perceive that whatever goes into the man from outside can’t defile him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because it doesn’t go into his heart, but into his stomach, then into the latrine, thus purifying all foods ?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “That which proceeds out of the man, that defiles the man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For from within, out of the hearts of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, sexual sins, murders, thefts,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: covetings, wickedness, deceit, lustful desires, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, and foolishness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From there he arose, and went away into the borders of Tyre and Sidon. He entered into a house, and didn’t want anyone to know it, but he couldn’t escape notice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For a woman, whose little daughter had an unclean spirit, having heard of him, came and fell down at his feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the woman was a Greek, a Syrophoenician by race. She begged him that he would cast the demon out of her daughter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to her, “Let the children be filled first, for it is not appropriate to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But she answered him, “Yes, Lord. Yet even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to her, “For this saying, go your way. The demon has gone out of your daughter.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She went away to her house, and found the child having been laid on the bed, with the demon gone out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again he departed from the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and came to the sea of Galilee, through the middle of the region of Decapolis.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought to him one who was deaf and had an impediment in his speech. They begged him to lay his hand on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took him aside from the multitude, privately, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spat, and touched his tongue.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Looking up to heaven, he sighed, and said to him, “Ephphatha!”  that is, “Be opened!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately his ears were opened, and the impediment of his tongue was released, and he spoke clearly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He commanded them that they should tell no one, but the more he commanded them, so much the more widely they proclaimed it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were astonished beyond measure, saying, “He has done all things well. He makes even the deaf hear, and the mute speak!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In those days, when there was a very great multitude, and they had nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples to himself, and said to them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I have compassion on the multitude, because they have stayed with me now three days, and have nothing to eat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If I send them away fasting to their home, they will faint on the way, for some of them have come a long way.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His disciples answered him, “From where could one satisfy these people with bread here in a deserted place?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He asked them, “How many loaves do you have?” They said, “Seven.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground, and he took the seven loaves. Having given thanks, he broke them, and gave them to his disciples to serve, and they served the multitude.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They had a few small fish. Having blessed them, he said to serve these also.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They ate, and were filled. They took up seven baskets of broken pieces that were left over.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who had eaten were about four thousand. Then he sent them away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately he entered into the boat with his disciples, and came into the region of Dalmanutha.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees came out and began to question him, seeking from him a sign from heaven, and testing him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sighed deeply in his spirit, and said, “Why does this generation   seek a sign? Most certainly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He left them, and again entering into the boat, departed to the other side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They forgot to take bread; and they didn’t have more than one loaf in the boat with them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He warned them, saying, “Take heed: beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They reasoned with one another, saying, “It’s because we have no bread.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus, perceiving it, said to them, “Why do you reason that it’s because you have no bread? Don’t you perceive yet, neither understand? Is your heart still hardened?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Having eyes, don’t you see? Having ears, don’t you hear? Don’t you remember?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When I broke the five loaves among the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up?” They told him, “Twelve.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “When the seven loaves fed the four thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up?” They told him, “Seven.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He asked them, “Don’t you understand, yet?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came to Bethsaida. They brought a blind man to him, and begged him to touch him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took hold of the blind man by the hand, and brought him out of the village. When he had spit on his eyes, and laid his hands on him, he asked him if he saw anything.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He looked up, and said, “I see men; for I see them like trees walking.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then again he laid his hands on his eyes. He looked intently, and was restored, and saw everyone clearly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sent him away to his house, saying, “Don’t enter into the village, nor tell anyone in the village.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus went out, with his disciples, into the villages of Caesarea Philippi. On the way he asked his disciples, “Who do men say that I am?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They told him, “John the Baptizer, and others say Elijah, but others: one of the prophets.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered, “You are the Christ.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He commanded them that they should tell no one about him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He spoke to them openly. Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he, turning around, and seeing his disciples, rebuked Peter, and said, “Get behind me, Satan! For you have in mind not the things of God, but the things of men.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called the multitude to himself with his disciples, and said to them, “Whoever wants to come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it; and whoever will lose his life for my sake and the sake of the Good News will save it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For what does it profit a man, to gain the whole world, and forfeit his life?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For what will a man give in exchange for his life?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man also will be ashamed of him, when he comes in his Father’s glory, with the holy angels.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, there are some standing here who will in no way taste death until they see God’s Kingdom come with power.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After six days Jesus took with him Peter, James, and John, and brought them up onto a high mountain privately by themselves, and he was changed into another form in front of them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His clothing became glistening, exceedingly white, like snow, such as no launderer on earth can whiten them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elijah and Moses appeared to them, and they were talking with Jesus.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Peter answered Jesus, “Rabbi, it is good for us to be here. Let’s make three tents: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he didn’t know what to say, for they were very afraid.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A cloud came, overshadowing them, and a voice came out of the cloud, “This is my beloved Son. Listen to him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Suddenly looking around, they saw no one with them any more, except Jesus only.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they were coming down from the mountain, he commanded them that they should tell no one what things they had seen, until after the Son of Man had risen from the dead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They kept this saying to themselves, questioning what the “rising from the dead” meant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They asked him, saying, “Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Elijah indeed comes first, and restores all things. How is it written about the Son of Man, that he should suffer many things and be despised?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I tell you that Elijah has come, and they have also done to him whatever they wanted to, even as it is written about him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Coming to the disciples, he saw a great multitude around them, and scribes questioning them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately all the multitude, when they saw him, were greatly amazed, and running to him greeted him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He asked the scribes, “What are you asking them?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One of the multitude answered, “Teacher, I brought to you my son, who has a mute spirit;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and wherever it seizes him, it throws him down, and he foams at the mouth, and grinds his teeth, and wastes away. I asked your disciples to cast it out, and they weren’t able.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered him, “Unbelieving generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I bear with you? Bring him to me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought him to him, and when he saw him, immediately the spirit convulsed him, and he fell on the ground, wallowing and foaming at the mouth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He asked his father, “How long has it been since this has come to him?” He said, “From childhood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Often it has cast him both into the fire and into the water, to destroy him. But if you can do anything, have compassion on us, and help us.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, “If you can believe, all things are possible to him who believes.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately the father of the child cried out with tears, “I believe. Help my unbelief!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jesus saw that a multitude came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to him, “You mute and deaf spirit, I command you, come out of him, and never enter him again!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Having cried out, and convulsed greatly, it came out of him. The boy became like one dead; so much that most of them said, “He is dead.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus took him by the hand, and raised him up; and he arose.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, “Why couldn’t we cast it out?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “This kind can come out by nothing, except by prayer and fasting.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went out from there, and passed through Galilee. He didn’t want anyone to know it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he was teaching his disciples, and said to them, “The Son of Man is being handed over to the hands of men, and they will kill him; and when he is killed, on the third day he will rise again.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But they didn’t understand the saying, and were afraid to ask him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came to Capernaum, and when he was in the house he asked them, “What were you arguing among yourselves on the way?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But they were silent, for they had disputed one with another on the way about who was the greatest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sat down, and called the twelve; and he said to them, “If any man wants to be first, he shall be last of all, and servant of all.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took a little child, and set him in the middle of them. Taking him in his arms, he said to them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Whoever receives one such little child in my name, receives me, and whoever receives me, doesn’t receive me, but him who sent me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: John said to him, “Teacher, we saw someone who doesn’t follow us casting out demons in your name; and we forbade him, because he doesn’t follow us.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said, “Don’t forbid him, for there is no one who will do a mighty work in my name, and be able quickly to speak evil of me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For whoever is not against us is on our side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For whoever will give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because you are Christ’s, most certainly I tell you, he will in no way lose his reward.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever will cause one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him if he were thrown into the sea with a millstone hung around his neck.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If your hand causes you to stumble, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life maimed, rather than having your two hands to go into Gehenna,   into the unquenchable fire,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life lame, rather than having your two feet to be cast into Gehenna,   into the fire that will never be quenched —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If your eye causes you to stumble, cast it out. It is better for you to enter into God’s Kingdom with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into the Gehenna  of fire,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For everyone will be salted with fire, and every sacrifice will be seasoned with salt.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Salt is good, but if the salt has lost its saltiness, with what will you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He arose from there and came into the borders of Judea and beyond the Jordan. Multitudes came together to him again. As he usually did, he was again teaching them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pharisees came to him testing him, and asked him, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered, “What did Moses command you?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said, “Moses allowed a certificate of divorce to be written, and to divorce her.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to them, “For your hardness of heart, he wrote you this commandment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But from the beginning of the creation, God made them male and female.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For this cause a man will leave his father and mother, and will join to his wife,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the two will become one flesh,  so that they are no longer two, but one flesh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What therefore God has joined together, let no man separate.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the house, his disciples asked him again about the same matter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Whoever divorces his wife, and marries another, commits adultery against her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If a woman herself divorces her husband, and marries another, she commits adultery.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were bringing to him little children, that he should touch them, but the disciples rebuked those who were bringing them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when Jesus saw it, he was moved with indignation, and said to them, “Allow the little children to come to me! Don’t forbid them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Most certainly I tell you, whoever will not receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took them in his arms, and blessed them, laying his hands on them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he was going out into the way, one ran to him, knelt before him, and asked him, “Good Teacher, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good except one — God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You know the commandments: ‘Do not murder,’ ‘Do not commit adultery,’ ‘Do not steal,’ ‘Do not give false testimony,’ ‘Do not defraud,’ ‘Honor your father and mother.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him, “Teacher, I have observed all these things from my youth.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus looking at him loved him, and said to him, “One thing you lack. Go, sell whatever you have, and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me, taking up the cross.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But his face fell at that saying, and he went away sorrowful, for he was one who had great possessions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus looked around, and said to his disciples, “How difficult it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus answered again, “Children, how hard is it for those who trust in riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were exceedingly astonished, saying to him, “Then who can be saved?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus, looking at them, said, “With men it is impossible, but not with God, for all things are possible with God.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Peter began to tell him, “Behold, we have left all, and have followed you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said, “Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or land, for my sake, and for the sake of the Good News,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but he will receive one hundred times more now in this time, houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and land, with persecutions; and in the age to come eternal life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But many who are first will be last; and the last first.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were on the way, going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus was going in front of them, and they were amazed; and those who followed were afraid. He again took the twelve, and began to tell them the things that were going to happen to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and the scribes. They will condemn him to death, and will deliver him to the Gentiles.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will mock him, spit on him, scourge him, and kill him. On the third day he will rise again.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came near to him, saying, “Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we will ask.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “What do you want me to do for you?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said to him, “Grant to us that we may sit, one at your right hand, and one at your left hand, in your glory.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to them, “You don’t know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said to him, “We are able.” Jesus said to them, “You shall indeed drink the cup that I drink, and you shall be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but to sit at my right hand and at my left hand is not mine to give, but for whom it has been prepared.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the ten heard it, they began to be indignant towards James and John.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus summoned them, and said to them, “You know that they who are recognized as rulers over the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But it shall not be so among you, but whoever wants to become great among you shall be your servant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever of you wants to become first among you, shall be bondservant of all.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Son of Man also came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came to Jericho. As he went out from Jericho, with his disciples and a great multitude, the son of Timaeus, Bartimaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting by the road.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he heard that it was Jesus the Nazarene, he began to cry out, and say, “Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Many rebuked him, that he should be quiet, but he cried out much more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus stood still, and said, “Call him.” They called the blind man, saying to him, “Cheer up! Get up. He is calling you!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He, casting away his cloak, sprang up, and came to Jesus.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus asked him, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man said to him, “Rabboni,  that I may see again.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, “Go your way. Your faith has made you well.”  Immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus on the way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they came near to Jerusalem, to Bethsphage  and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and said to them, “Go your way into the village that is opposite you. Immediately as you enter into it, you will find a young donkey tied, on which no one has sat. Untie him, and bring him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ say, ‘The Lord needs him;’ and immediately he will send him back here.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went away, and found a young donkey tied at the door outside in the open street, and they untied him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Some of those who stood there asked them, “What are you doing, untying the young donkey?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said to them just as Jesus had said, and they let them go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought the young donkey to Jesus, and threw their garments on it, and Jesus sat on it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Many spread their garments on the way, and others were cutting down branches from the trees, and spreading them on the road.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who went in front, and those who followed, cried out, “Hosanna!  Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is coming in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus entered into the temple in Jerusalem. When he had looked around at everything, it being now evening, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The next day, when they had come out from Bethany, he was hungry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came to see if perhaps he might find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus told it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again!”  and his disciples heard it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came to Jerusalem, and Jesus entered into the temple, and began to throw out those who sold and those who bought in the temple, and overthrew the money changers’ tables, and the seats of those who sold the doves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He would not allow anyone to carry a container through the temple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He taught, saying to them, “Isn’t it written, ‘My house will be called a house of prayer for all the nations?’  But you have made it a den of robbers!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The chief priests and the scribes heard it, and sought how they might destroy him. For they feared him, because all the multitude was astonished at his teaching.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When evening came, he went out of the city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they passed by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered away from the roots.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Peter, remembering, said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree which you cursed has withered away.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For most certainly I tell you, whoever may tell this mountain, ‘Be taken up and cast into the sea,’ and doesn’t doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says is happening; he shall have whatever he says.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore I tell you, all things whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you have received them, and you shall have them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father, who is in heaven, may also forgive you your transgressions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your transgressions.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came again to Jerusalem, and as he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders came to him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they began saying to him, “By what authority do you do these things? Or who gave you this authority to do these things?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question. Answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The baptism of John — was it from heaven, or from men? Answer me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we should say, ‘From heaven;’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If we should say, ‘From men’” — they feared the people, for all held John to really be a prophet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” Jesus said to them, “Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard, put a hedge around it, dug a pit for the wine press, built a tower, rented it out to a farmer, and went into another country.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When it was time, he sent a servant to the farmer to get from the farmer his share of the fruit of the vineyard.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They took him, beat him, and sent him away empty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again, he sent another servant to them; and they threw stones at him, wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully treated.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again he sent another; and they killed him; and many others, beating some, and killing some.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore still having one, his beloved son, he sent him last to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But those farmers said among themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They took him, killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the farmers, and will give the vineyard to others.

translations.name: World English Bible
