translations.text: Haven’t you even read this Scripture:  ‘The stone which the builders rejected,  the same was made the head of the corner.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This was from the Lord,  it is marvelous in our eyes’?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They tried to seize him, but they feared the multitude; for they perceived that he spoke the parable against them. They left him, and went away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They sent some of the Pharisees and of the Herodians to him, that they might trap him with words.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had come, they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest, and don’t defer to anyone; for you aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Shall we give, or shall we not give?” But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said to them, “Why do you test me? Bring me a denarius, that I may see it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought it. He said to them, “Whose is this image and inscription?” They said to him, “Caesar’s.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, “Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.” They marveled greatly at him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There came to him Sadducees, who say that there is no resurrection. They asked him, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Teacher, Moses wrote to us, ‘If a man’s brother dies, and leaves a wife behind him, and leaves no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up offspring for his brother.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were seven brothers. The first took a wife, and dying left no offspring.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The second took her, and died, leaving no children behind him. The third likewise;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the seven took her and left no children. Last of all the woman also died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the resurrection, when they rise, whose wife will she be of them? For the seven had her as a wife.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, “Isn’t this because you are mistaken, not knowing the Scriptures, nor the power of God?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For when they will rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But about the dead, that they are raised; haven’t you read in the book of Moses, about the Bush, how God spoke to him, saying, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are therefore badly mistaken.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One of the scribes came, and heard them questioning together. Knowing that he had answered them well, asked him, “Which commandment is the greatest of all?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, “The greatest is, ‘Hear, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.’  This is the first commandment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The second is like this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’  There is no other commandment greater than these.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The scribe said to him, “Truly, teacher, you have said well that he is one, and there is none other but he,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbor as himself, is more important than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from God’s Kingdom.” No one dared ask him any question after that.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus responded, as he taught in the temple, “How is it that the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For David himself said in the Holy Spirit,  ‘The Lord said to my Lord,  “Sit at my right hand,  until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be his son?”  The common people heard him gladly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In his teaching he said to them, “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk in long robes, and to get greetings in the marketplaces,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: those who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus sat down opposite the treasury, and saw how the multitude cast money into the treasury. Many who were rich cast in much.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A poor widow came, and she cast in two small brass coins,  which equal a quadrans coin.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called his disciples to himself, and said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, this poor widow gave more than all those who are giving into the treasury,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for they all gave out of their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, gave all that she had to live on.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he went out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, see what kind of stones and what kind of buildings!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? There will not be left here one stone on another, which will not be thrown down.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are all about to be fulfilled?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus, answering, began to tell them, “Be careful that no one leads you astray.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he!’  and will lead many astray.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, don’t be troubled. For those must happen, but the end is not yet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places. There will be famines and troubles. These things are the beginning of birth pains.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But watch yourselves, for they will deliver you up to councils. You will be beaten in synagogues. You will stand before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Good News must first be preached to all the nations.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they lead you away and deliver you up, don’t be anxious beforehand, or premeditate what you will say, but say whatever will be given you in that hour. For it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Brother will deliver up brother to death, and the father his child. Children will rise up against parents, and cause them to be put to death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake, but he who endures to the end, the same will be saved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when you see the abomination of desolation,  spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not (let the reader understand), then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and let him who is on the housetop not go down, nor enter in, to take anything out of his house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let him who is in the field not return back to take his cloak.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But woe to those who are with child and to those who nurse babies in those days!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pray that your flight won’t be in the winter.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For in those days there will be oppression, such as there has not been the like from the beginning of the creation which God created until now, and never will be.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Unless the Lord had shortened the days, no flesh would have been saved; but for the sake of the chosen ones, whom he picked out, he shortened the days.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then if anyone tells you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or, ‘Look, there!’ don’t believe it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For there will arise false christs and false prophets, and will show signs and wonders, that they may lead astray, if possible, even the chosen ones.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But you watch.  “Behold, I have told you all things beforehand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But in those days, after that oppression, the sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the stars will be falling from the sky, and the powers that are in the heavens will be shaken.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he will send out his angels, and will gather together his chosen ones from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of the sky.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now from the fig tree, learn this parable. When the branch has now become tender, and produces its leaves, you know that the summer is near;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: even so you also, when you see these things coming to pass, know that it is near, at the doors.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Most certainly I say to you, this generation  will not pass away until all these things happen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But of that day or that hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Watch, keep alert, and pray; for you don’t know when the time is.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “It is like a man, traveling to another country, having left his house, and given authority to his servants, and to each one his work, and also commanded the doorkeeper to keep watch.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Watch therefore, for you don’t know when the lord of the house is coming, whether at evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: lest coming suddenly he might find you sleeping.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What I tell you, I tell all: Watch.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It was now two days before the feast of the Passover and the unleavened bread, and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might seize him by deception, and kill him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they said, “Not during the feast, because there might be a riot of the people.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While he was at Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at the table, a woman came having an alabaster jar of ointment of pure nard — very costly. She broke the jar, and poured it over his head.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But there were some who were indignant among themselves, saying, “Why has this ointment been wasted?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For this might have been sold for more than three hundred denarii,  and given to the poor.” They grumbled against her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. Why do you trouble her? She has done a good work for me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you want to, you can do them good; but you will not always have me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body beforehand for the burying.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Most certainly I tell you, wherever this Good News may be preached throughout the whole world, that which this woman has done will also be spoken of for a memorial of her.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went away to the chief priests, that he might deliver him to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They, when they heard it, were glad, and promised to give him money. He sought how he might conveniently deliver him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the first day of unleavened bread, when they sacrificed the Passover, his disciples asked him, “Where do you want us to go and prepare that you may eat the Passover?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sent two of his disciples, and said to them, “Go into the city, and there you will meet a man carrying a pitcher of water. Follow him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and wherever he enters in, tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will himself show you a large upper room furnished and ready. Get ready for us there.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His disciples went out, and came into the city, and found things as he had said to them, and they prepared the Passover.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When it was evening he came with the twelve.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they sat and were eating, Jesus said, “Most certainly I tell you, one of you will betray me — he who eats with me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They began to be sorrowful, and to ask him one by one, “Surely not I?” And another said, “Surely not I?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered them, “It is one of the twelve, he who dips with me in the dish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Son of Man goes, even as it is written about him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would be better for that man if he had not been born.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they were eating, Jesus took bread, and when he had blessed, he broke it, and gave to them, and said, “Take, eat. This is my body.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave to them. They all drank of it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “This is my blood of the new covenant, which is poured out for many.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Most certainly I tell you, I will no more drink of the fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it anew in God’s Kingdom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to them, “All of you will be made to stumble because of me tonight, for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However, after I am raised up, I will go before you into Galilee.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Peter said to him, “Although all will be offended, yet I will not.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, “Most certainly I tell you, that you today, even this night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he spoke all the more, “If I must die with you, I will not deny you.” They all said the same thing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came to a place which was named Gethsemane. He said to his disciples, “Sit here, while I pray.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took with him Peter, James, and John, and began to be greatly troubled and distressed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here, and watch.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went forward a little, and fell on the ground, and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass away from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Abba,  Father, all things are possible to you. Please remove this cup from me. However, not what I desire, but what you desire.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, “Simon, are you sleeping? Couldn’t you watch one hour?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Watch and pray, that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again he went away, and prayed, saying the same words.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again he returned, and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy, and they didn’t know what to answer him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came the third time, and said to them, “Sleep on now, and take your rest. It is enough. The hour has come. Behold, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Arise, let us be going. Behold, he who betrays me is at hand.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, came — and with him a multitude with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now he who betrayed him had given them a sign, saying, “Whomever I will kiss, that is he. Seize him, and lead him away safely.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had come, immediately he came to him, and said, “Rabbi! Rabbi!” and kissed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They laid their hands on him, and seized him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But a certain one of those who stood by drew his sword, and struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, “Have you come out, as against a robber, with swords and clubs to seize me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and you didn’t arrest me. But this is so that the Scriptures might be fulfilled.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They all left him, and fled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A certain young man followed him, having a linen cloth thrown around himself, over his naked body. The young men grabbed him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but he left the linen cloth, and fled from them naked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They led Jesus away to the high priest. All the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes came together with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Peter had followed him from a distance, until he came into the court of the high priest. He was sitting with the officers, and warming himself in the light of the fire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the chief priests and the whole council sought witnesses against Jesus to put him to death, and found none.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For many gave false testimony against him, and their testimony didn’t agree with each other.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Some stood up, and gave false testimony against him, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another made without hands.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Even so, their testimony did not agree.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The high priest stood up in the middle, and asked Jesus, “Have you no answer? What is it which these testify against you?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he stayed quiet, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, “Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said, “I am. You will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power, and coming with the clouds of the sky.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The high priest tore his clothes, and said, “What further need have we of witnesses?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You have heard the blasphemy! What do you think?” They all condemned him to be worthy of death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to beat him with fists, and to tell him, “Prophesy!” The officers struck him with the palms of their hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As Peter was in the courtyard below, one of the maids of the high priest came,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him, and said, “You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he denied it, saying, “I neither know, nor understand what you are saying.” He went out on the porch, and the rooster crowed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The maid saw him, and began again to tell those who stood by, “This is one of them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he again denied it. After a little while again those who stood by said to Peter, “You truly are one of them, for you are a Galilean, and your speech shows it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he began to curse, and to swear, “I don’t know this man of whom you speak!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The rooster crowed the second time. Peter remembered the word, how that Jesus said to him, “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.” When he thought about that, he wept.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately in the morning the chief priests, with the elders and scribes, and the whole council, held a consultation, and bound Jesus, and carried him away, and delivered him up to Pilate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered, “So you say.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The chief priests accused him of many things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pilate again asked him, “Have you no answer? See how many things they testify against you!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus made no further answer, so that Pilate marveled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now at the feast he used to release to them one prisoner, whom they asked of him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was one called Barabbas, bound with his fellow insurgents, men who in the insurrection had committed murder.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The multitude, crying aloud, began to ask him to do as he always did for them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pilate answered them, saying, “Do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he perceived that for envy the chief priests had delivered him up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the chief priests stirred up the multitude, that he should release Barabbas to them instead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pilate again asked them, “What then should I do to him whom you call the King of the Jews?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They cried out again, “Crucify him!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pilate said to them, “Why, what evil has he done?” But they cried out exceedingly, “Crucify him!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pilate, wishing to please the multitude, released Barabbas to them, and handed over Jesus, when he had flogged him, to be crucified.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The soldiers led him away within the court, which is the Praetorium; and they called together the whole cohort.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They clothed him with purple, and weaving a crown of thorns, they put it on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They began to salute him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They struck his head with a reed, and spat on him, and bowing their knees, did homage to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had mocked him, they took the purple off of him, and put his own garments on him. They led him out to crucify him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They compelled one passing by, coming from the country, Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to go with them, that he might bear his cross.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought him to the place called Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, “The place of a skull.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They offered him wine mixed with myrrh to drink, but he didn’t take it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Crucifying him, they parted his garments among them, casting lots on them, what each should take.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It was the third hour,  and they crucified him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The superscription of his accusation was written over him, “THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: With him they crucified two robbers; one on his right hand, and one on his left.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Scripture was fulfilled, which says, “He was counted with transgressors.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who passed by blasphemed him, wagging their heads, and saying, “Ha! You who destroy the temple, and build it in three days,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: save yourself, and come down from the cross!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Likewise, also the chief priests mocking among themselves with the scribes said, “He saved others. He can’t save himself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let the Christ, the King of Israel, now come down from the cross, that we may see and believe him.”  Those who were crucified with him also insulted him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the sixth hour  had come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, “Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?” which is, being interpreted, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Some of those who stood by, when they heard it, said, “Behold, he is calling Elijah.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One ran, and filling a sponge full of vinegar, put it on a reed, and gave it to him to drink, saying, “Let him be. Let’s see whether Elijah comes to take him down.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus cried out with a loud voice, and gave up the spirit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The veil of the temple was torn in two from the top to the bottom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the centurion, who stood by opposite him, saw that he cried out like this and breathed his last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were also women watching from afar, among whom were both Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who, when he was in Galilee, followed him, and served him; and many other women who came up with him to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When evening had now come, because it was the Preparation Day, that is, the day before the Sabbath,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joseph of Arimathaea, a prominent council member who also himself was looking for God’s Kingdom, came. He boldly went in to Pilate, and asked for Jesus’ body.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Pilate marveled if he were already dead; and summoning the centurion, he asked him whether he had been dead long.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he found out from the centurion, he granted the body to Joseph.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He bought a linen cloth, and taking him down, wound him in the linen cloth, and laid him in a tomb which had been cut out of a rock. He rolled a stone against the door of the tomb.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mary Magdalene and Mary, the mother of Joses, saw where he was laid.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, bought spices, that they might come and anoint him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Very early on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb when the sun had risen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were saying among themselves, “Who will roll away the stone from the door of the tomb for us?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for it was very big. Looking up, they saw that the stone was rolled back.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Entering into the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed in a white robe, and they were amazed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Don’t be amazed. You seek Jesus, the Nazarene, who has been crucified. He has risen. He is not here. Behold, the place where they laid him!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But go, tell his disciples and Peter, ‘He goes before you into Galilee. There you will see him, as he said to you.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went out,  and fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment had come on them. They said nothing to anyone; for they were afraid.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when he had risen early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She went and told those who had been with him, as they mourned and wept.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they heard that he was alive, and had been seen by her, they disbelieved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After these things he was revealed in another form to two of them, as they walked, on their way into the country.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went away and told it to the rest. They didn’t believe them, either.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Afterward he was revealed to the eleven themselves as they sat at the table, and he rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they didn’t believe those who had seen him after he had risen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Go into all the world, and preach the Good News to the whole creation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who believes and is baptized will be saved; but he who disbelieves will be condemned.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These signs will accompany those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons; they will speak with new languages;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: they will take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it will in no way hurt them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So then the Lord,  after he had spoken to them, was received up into heaven, and sat down at the right hand of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They went out, and preached everywhere, the Lord working with them, and confirming the word by the signs that followed. Amen.
